title: Sedimentation in the Chile Trench: Depositional morphologies, lithofacies, and stratigraphy
abstract: The depositional bodies of the Chile Trench (trench fans, axial channel, sheeted basins, ponded basins, and axial sediment lobes) control the spatial distribution of modern lithofacies in the basin. The Chile Trench is divided into three physiographic provinces: North Chile (18°–27.5°S), Central Chile (27.5°–33°S), and South Chile (33°–45°S). Five lithofacies are defined by Q-mode factor analysis of sediment textures in 27 piston cores. The trench wedge records a coarsening-upward sequence as the oceanic plate migrates toward the subduction zone. Single-channel seismic reflection and 3.5 kHz subbottom profiler data image trench fans (Bio Bio Fan at ~37°S, Callecalle Fan at ~40°S), the axial channel, and sheeted/ponded basins throughout the margin.

location.description: Chile Trench axial sedimentation 18°–45°S (full trench length); single-channel seismic reflection, piston cores and bathymetry

seismic_lines.name: Oregon State University SCS / 3.5 kHz profiles — Chile Trench 18°–45°S

data.name: OSU Single-Channel Seismic / 3.5 kHz — Chile Trench 18°–45°S
data.description: Single-channel seismic reflection and 3.5 kHz subbottom profiles from OSU cruises along the Chile Trench axis (18°–45°S); images Bio Bio Fan (~37°S), Callecalle Fan (~40°S), axial channel, and sheeted/ponded basins; foundational trench sedimentation study (NSF OCE-7919361)

data.name: R/V Yaquina Bathymetry — Chile Trench 36°–46°S
data.description: Bathymetric data from R/V Yaquina cruises mapping Chile Trench depositional morphology 36°–46°S

_data_note: Classic 1987 GSAB paper; behind paywall. R/V Yaquina (Oregon State) legacy data. Repository uncertain; may be at NGDC/NOAA or MGDS.

processing.summary: Single-channel seismic reflection profiles, piston cores, and bathymetric data from R/V Yaquina cruises are used to characterize Chile Trench axial sedimentation from 36°S to 46°S. Depositional morphologies (turbidite systems, ponded sediments, inter-ridge fans) and lithofacies variations are described in relation to terrigenous input sources.
processing.notes: Classic foundational paper on Chile Trench sedimentation; legacy Oregon State University (College of Oceanography) data. R/V Melville bathymetry data from 1980 explicitly cited (Fig. 3 caption). R/V Yaquina (OSU vessel) is probable primary source for SCS profiles but not explicitly named in accessible pages. Q-mode factor analysis by Klovan & Imbrie (1971) algorithm for lithofacies. NSF grant OCE-7919361.

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. Authors expanded to full names: Thornburg Todd M., Kulm Laverne D. (Oregon State University, College of Oceanography). bounding_box: lat_max -36 → -18 (paper covers entire Chile Trench from 18°S–45°S, not just 36°–46°S). abstract rewritten to reflect actual paper content. source_type: removed 'airgun array', added '3.5 kHz subbottom profiler' (explicitly stated in Fig 10 caption). data_type: 'seismic_reflection_mcs' → 'seismic_reflection_scs' (single-channel). software: 'unknown' → [] (none cited). R/V Melville confirmed for 1980 bathymetry data; R/V Yaquina (OSU) probable but not confirmed in accessible text. NSF OCE-7919361 funding confirmed.
